apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rulesAlerts []*rules.Alert) []*Alert {\n\tapiAlerts := make([]*Alert, len(rulesAlerts))\n\tfor i, ruleAlert := range rulesAlerts {\n\t\tapiAlerts[i] = &Alert{\n\t\t\tLabels:      ruleAlert.Labels,\n\t\t\tAnnotations: ruleAlert.Annotations,\n\t\t\tState:       ruleAlert.State.String(),\n\t\t\tActiveAt:    &ruleAlert.ActiveAt,\n\t\t\tValue:       strconv.FormatFloat(ruleAlert.Value, 'e', -1, 64),\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tapiAlerts[i].KeepFiringSince = &ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiAlerts\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) metricMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tmetrics := map[string]map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tlimitPerMetric := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit_per_metric\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limitPerMetric, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit_per_metric must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n\tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n\t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, mm := range t.ListMetadata() {\n\t\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: mm.Type, Help: mm.Help, Unit: mm.Unit}\n\t\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[mm.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[mm.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: md.Type, Help: md.Help, Unit: md.Unit}\n\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[md.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[md.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n\tres := map[string][]metadata.Metadata{}\n\tfor name, set := range metrics {\n\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ts := []metadata.Metadata{}\n\t\tfor metadata := range set {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, metadata)\n\t\t}\n\t\tres[name] = s\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\ntype RuleDiscovery struct {\n\tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n\tGroupNextToken string       `json:\"groupNextToken,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// RuleGroup has info for rules which are part of a group.\ntype RuleGroup struct {\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\tFile string `json:\"file\"`\n\t// In order to preserve rule ordering, while exposing type (alerting or recording)\n\t// specific properties, both alerting and recording rules are exposed in the\n\t// same array.\n\tRules          []Rule    `json:\"rules\"`\n\tInterval       float64   `json:\"interval\"`\n\tLimit          int       `json:\"limit\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64   `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n}\n\ntype Rule any\n\ntype AlertingRule struct {\n\t// State can be \"pending\", \"firing\", \"inactive\".\n\tState          string           `json:\"state\"`\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tDuration       float64          `json:\"duration\"`\n\tKeepFiringFor  float64          `json:\"keepFiringFor\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels\"`\n\tAnnotations    labels.Labels    `json:\"annotations\"`\n\tAlerts         []*Alert         `json:\"alerts\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of an alertingRule is always \"alerting\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\ntype RecordingRule struct {\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of a recordingRule is always \"recording\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) rules(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tqueryFormToSet := func(values []string) map[string]struct{} {\n\t\tset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(values))\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tset[v] = struct{}{}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn set\n\t}\n\n\trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n\trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n\tfSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"file[]\"])\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\truleGroups := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).RuleGroups()\n\tres := &RuleDiscovery{RuleGroups: make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))}\n\ttyp := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\"))\n\n\tif typ != \"\" && typ != \"alert\" && typ != \"record\" {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"not supported value %q\", typ), \"type\")\n\t}\n\n\treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n\treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n\t}\n\n\tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r)\n\tif parseErr != nil {\n\t\treturn *parseErr\n\t}\n\n\trgs := make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))\n\n\tfoundToken := false\n\n\tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfoundToken = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(fSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := fSet[grp.File()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tapiRuleGroup := &RuleGroup{\n\t\t\tName:           grp.Name(),\n\t\t\tFile:           grp.File(),\n\t\t\tInterval:       grp.Interval().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLimit:          grp.Limit(),\n\t\t\tRules:          []Rule{},\n\t\t\tEvaluationTime: grp.GetEvaluationTime().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLastEvaluation: grp.GetLastEvaluation(),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n\t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n\n\t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n\t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts []*Alert\n\t\t\t\tif !excludeAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tactiveAlerts = rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rule.ActiveAlerts())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = AlertingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tState:          rule.State().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tDuration:       rule.HoldDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tKeepFiringFor:  rule.KeepFiringFor().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAnnotations:    rule.Annotations(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAlerts:         activeAlerts,\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"alerting\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcase *rules.RecordingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnRecording {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = RecordingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"recording\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"failed to assert type of rule '%v'\", rule.Name())\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n\t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n\t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n\t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. Therefore a next token is required.\n\t\t\t\tres.GroupNextToken = getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name())\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trgs = append(rgs, apiRuleGroup)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"invalid group_next_token '%v'. were rule groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n\t}\n\n\tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n\texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n\n\tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := strconv.ParseBool(excludeAlertsParam)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"error converting exclude_alerts: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn excludeAlerts, nil\n}\n\nfunc parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r *http.Request) (int64, string, *apiFuncResult) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups int64 = -1\n\t\terr             error\n\t)\n\tmaxGroups := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_limit\")\n\tnextToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_next_token\")\n\n\tif nextToken != \"\" && maxGroups == \"\" {\n\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be present in order to paginate over the hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n}\n\ntype prometheusConfig struct {\n\tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstatus, err := (api *API) notificationsSSE(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"keep-alive\")\n\n\t// Subscribe to notifications.\n\tnotifications, unsubscribe, ok := api.notificationsSub()\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer unsubscribe()\n\n\t// Set up a flusher to push the response to the client.\n\tflusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Streaming unsupported\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Flush the response to ensure the headers are immediately and eventSource\n\t// onopen is triggered client-side.\n\tflusher.Flush()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase notification := <-notifications:\n\t\t\t// Marshal the notification to JSON.\n\t\t\tjsonData, err := json.Marshal(notification)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Write the event data in SSE format with JSON content.\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \"data: %s\\n\\n\", jsonData)\n\n\t\t\t// Flush the response to ensure the data is sent immediately.\n\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n\t\tcase <-r.Context().Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) remoteRead(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// This is only really for tests - this will never be nil IRL.\n\tif deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif !api.enableAdmin {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif err := *API) snapshot(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif !api.enableAdmin {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tvar (\n\t\tskipHead bool\n\t\terr      error\n\t)\n\tif r.FormValue(\"skip_head\") != \"\" {\n\t\tskipHead, err = strconv.ParseBool(r.FormValue(\"skip_head\"))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn string is needed to get the position information for the annotations, and it\n// can be empty if the position information isn't needed.\nfunc (api *API) respond(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, data any, warnings annotations.Annotations, query string) {\n\tstatusMessage := statusSuccess\n\twarn, info := warnings.AsStrings(query, 10, 10)\n\n\tresp := &Response{\n\t\tStatus:   statusMessage,\n\t\tData:     data,\n\t\tWarnings: warn,\n\t\tInfos:    info,\n\t}\n\n\tcodec, err := api.negotiateCodec(req, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tapi.respondError(w, &apiError{errorNotAcceptable, err}, nil)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tb, err := codec.Encode(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error marshaling response\", \"url\", req.URL, \"err\", err)\n\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", codec.ContentType().String())\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\n\t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error writing response\", \"url\", req.URL, \"bytesWritten\", n, \"err\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) negotiateCodec(req *http.Request, resp *Response) (Codec, error) {\n\tfor _, clause := range goautoneg.ParseAccept(req.Header.Get(\"Accept\")) {\n\t\tfor _, codec := range api.codecs {\n\t\t\tif codec.ContentType().Satisfies(clause) && codec.CanEncode(resp) {\n\t\t\t\treturn codec, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdefaultCodec := api.codecs[0]\n\tif !defaultCodec.CanEncode(resp) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot encode response as %s\", defaultCodec.ContentType())\n\t}\n\n\treturn defaultCodec, nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\n\tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\n\tb, err := json.Marshal(&Response{\n\t\tStatus:    statusError,\n\t\tErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\n\t\tError:     apiErr.err.Error(),\n\t\tData:      data,\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error marshaling json response\", \"err\", err)\n\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar code int\n\tif api.overrideErrorCode != nil {\n\t\tif newCode, override := api.overrideErrorCode(apiErr.typ.num, apiErr.err); override {\n\t\t\tcode = newCode\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(code)\n\tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\n\t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error writing response\", \"bytesWritten\", n, \"err\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc getDefaultErrorCode(errType errorType) int {\n\tswitch errType {\n\tcase errorBadData:\n\t\treturn http.StatusBadRequest\n\tcase errorExec:\n\t\treturn http.StatusUnprocessableEntity\n\tcase errorCanceled:\n\t\treturn statusClientClosedConnection\n\tcase errorTimeout:\n\t\treturn http.StatusServiceUnavailable\n\tcase errorInternal:\n\t\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\n\tcase errorNotFound:\n\t\treturn http.StatusNotFound\n\tcase errorNotAcceptable:\n\t\treturn http.StatusNotAcceptable\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc parseTimeParam(r *http.Request, paramName string, defaultValue time.Time) (time.Time, error) {\n\tval := r.FormValue(paramName)\n\tif val == \"\" {\n\t\treturn defaultValue, nil\n\t}\n\tresult, err := parseTime(val)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid time value for '%s': %w\", paramName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\nfunc parseTime(s string) (time.Time, error) {\n\tif t, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\n\t\ts, ns := math.Modf(t)\n\t\tns = math.Round(ns*1000) / 1000\n\t\treturn time.Unix(int64(s), int64(ns*float64(time.Second))).UTC(), nil\n\t}\n\tif t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s); err == nil {\n\t\treturn t, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Stdlib's time parser can only handle 4 digit years. As a workaround until\n\t// that is fixed we want to at least support our own boundary times.\n\t// Context: https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang/issues/614\n\t// Upstream issue: https://github.com/golang/go/issues/20555\n\tswitch s {\n\tcase minTimeFormatted:\n\t\treturn MinTime, nil\n\tcase maxTimeFormatted:\n\t\treturn MaxTime, nil\n\t}\n\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid timestamp\", s)\n}\n\nfunc parseDuration(s string) (time.Duration, error) {\n\tif d, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\n\t\tts := d * float64(time.Second)\n\t\tif ts > float64(math.MaxInt64) || ts < float64(math.MinInt64) {\n\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration. It overflows int64\", s)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn time.Duration(ts), nil\n\t}\n\tif d, err := model.ParseDuration(s); err == nil {\n\t\treturn time.Duration(d), nil\n\t}\n\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration\", s)\n}\n\nfunc parseMatchersParam(matchers []string) ([][]*labels.Matcher, error) {\n\tmatcherSets, err := parser.ParseMetricSelectors(matchers)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\nOUTER:\n\tfor _, ms := range matcherSets {\n\t\tfor _, lm := range ms {\n\t\t\tif lm != nil && !lm.Matches(\"\") {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue OUTER\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"match[] must contain at least one non-empty matcher\")\n\t}\n\treturn matcherSets, nil\n}\n\n// parseLimitParam returning 0 means no limit is to be applied.\nfunc parseLimitParam(limitStr string) (limit int, err error) {\n\tif limitStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn limit, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlimit, err = strconv.Atoi(limitStr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn limit, err\n\t}\n\tif limit < 0 {\n\t\treturn limit, errors.New(\"limit must be non-negative\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn limit, nil\n}\n\n// toHintLimit increases the API limit, as returned by parseLimitParam, by 1.\n// This allows for emitting warnings when the results are truncated.\nfunc toHintLimit(limit int) int {\n\t// 0 means no limit and avoid int overflow\n\tif limit > 0 && limit < math.MaxInt {\n\t\treturn limit + 1\n\t}\n\treturn limit\n}\n\n// truncateResults truncates result for queryRange() and query().\n// No truncation for other types(Scalars or Strings).\nfunc truncateResults(result *promql.Result, limit int) (*promql.Result, bool) {\n\tisTruncated := false\n\n\tswitch v := result.Value.(type) {\n\tcase promql.Matrix:\n\t\tif len(v) > limit {\n\t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n\t\t\tisTruncated = true\n\t\t}\n\tcase promql.Vector:\n\t\tif len(v) > limit {\n\t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n\t\t\tisTruncated = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return the modified result. != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), ts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) formatQuery(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{expr.Pretty(0), nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) parseQuery(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: translateAST(expr), err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc extractQueryOpts(r *http.Request) (promql.QueryOpts, error) {\n\tvar duration time.Duration\n\n\tif strDuration := r.FormValue(\"lookback_delta\"); strDuration != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedDuration, err := parseDuration(strDuration)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing lookback delta duration: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tduration = parsedDuration\n\t}\n\n\treturn promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(r.FormValue(\"stats\") == \"all\", duration), nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryRange(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tstart, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"start\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"end\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start time\"), \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tstep, err := parseDuration(r.FormValue(\"step\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\tif step <= 0 {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"zero or negative query resolution step widths are not accepted. Try a positive integer\"), \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\n\t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\n\tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewRangeQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), start, end, step)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryExemplars(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tselectors := parser.ExtractSelectors(expr)\n\tif len(selectors) < 1 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\teq, err := api.ExemplarQueryable.ExemplarQuerier(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tres, err := eq.Select(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), selectors...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc returnAPIError(err error) *apiError {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar eqc promql.ErrQueryCanceled\n\tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\n\tvar es promql.ErrStorage\n\tswitch {\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &es):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\n\t}\n\n\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit, err := warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{names, nil, warnings, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMinTime = time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000+62135596801, 0).UTC()\n\n\t// MaxTime is the default timestamp used for the end of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects to reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a lower value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still high enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMaxTime = time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000-62135596801, 999999999).UTC()\n\n\tminTimeFormatted = MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\tmaxTimeFormatted = MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n)\n\nfunc (api *API) series(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif {\n\t\tq.Close()\n\t}\n\n\thints := &storage.SelectHints{\n\t\tStart: timestamp.FromTime(start),\n\t\tEnd:   timestamp.FromTime(end),\n\t\tFunc:  \"series\", // There is no series function, this token is used for lookups that don't need samples.\n\t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\tvar set storage.SeriesSet\n\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tvar sets []storage.SeriesSet\n\t\tfor _, mset := s)\n\t\t}\n\t\tset = storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(sets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// At this point at least one match exists.\n\t\tset = q.Select(ctx, false, hints, matcherSets[0]...)\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics := []labels.Labels{}\n\n\twarnings := set.Warnings()\n\n\ti := 1\n\tfor set.Next() {\n\t\tif i%checkContextEveryNIterations == 0 {\n\t\t\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, closer}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\ti++\n\n\t\tmetrics = append(metrics, set.At().Labels())\n\n\t\tif limit > 0 && len(metrics) > apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, errors.New(\"not implemented\")}, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// Target has the information for one target.\ntype Target struct {\n\t// Labels before any processing.\n\tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n\t// Any labels that are added to this target and its metrics.\n\tLabels labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n\n\tScrapePool string `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n\tScrapeURL  string `json:\"scrapeUrl\"`\n\tGlobalURL  string `json:\"globalUrl\"`\n\n\tLastError          string              `json:\"lastError\"`\n\tLastScrape         time.Time           `json:\"lastScrape\"`\n\tLastScrapeDuration float64             `json:\"lastScrapeDuration\"`\n\tHealth             scrape.TargetHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\n\tScrapeInterval string `json:\"scrapeInterval\"`\n\tScrapeTimeout  string `json:\"scrapeTimeout\"`\n}\n\ntype ScrapePoolsDiscovery struct {\n\tScrapePools []string `json:\"scrapePools\"`\n}\n\n// DroppedTarget has the information for one target that was dropped during relabelling.\ntype DroppedTarget struct {\n\t// Labels before any processing.\n\tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n\tScrapePool       string        `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n}\n\n// TargetDiscovery has all the active targets.\ntype TargetDiscovery struct {\n\tActiveTargets       []*Target        `json:\"activeTargets\"`\n\tDroppedTargets      []*DroppedTarget `json:\"droppedTargets\"`\n\tDroppedTargetCounts map[string]int   `json:\"droppedTargetCounts\"`\n}\n\n// GlobalURLOptions contains fields used for deriving the global URL for local targets.\ntype GlobalURLOptions struct {\n\tListenAddress string\n\tHost          string\n\tScheme        string\n}\n\n// sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\n// Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\n// original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\n// brackets.\nfunc sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\n\thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\n\tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \"missing port in address\") {\n\t\tvar errWithPort error\n\t\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \":80\")\n\t\tif errWithPort == nil {\n\t\t\terr = nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn host, port, err\n}\n\nfunc getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\n\thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn u, err\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\n\t\tif host == lhr {\n\t\t\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif port == ownPort {\n\t\t\t\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\n\t\t\t\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\n\t\t\t\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\n\t\t\t\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\n\t\t\t\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\n\t\t\t\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\n\t\t\t\t// should already contain the prefix.\n\t\t\t\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\n\t\t\t\tu.Host = opts.Host\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\n\t\t\t\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\n\t\t\t\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\n\t\t\t\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\n\t\t\t\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tu.Host = host\n\t\t\t\tif port != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tu.Host = net.JoinHostPort(u.Host, port)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn u, nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) scrapePools(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tnames := api.scrapePoolsRetriever(r.Context()).ScrapePools()\n\tsort.Strings(names)\n\tres := &ScrapePoolsDiscovery{ScrapePools: names}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: res, err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) targets(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tgetSortedPools := func(targets map[string][]*scrape.Target) ([]string, int) {\n\t\tvar n int\n\t\tpools := make([]string, 0, len(targets))\n\t\tfor p, t := range targets {\n\t\t\tpools = append(pools, p)\n\t\t\tn += len(t)\n\t\t}\n\t\tslices.Sort(pools)\n\t\treturn pools, n\n\t}\n\n\tscrapePool := r.URL.Query().Get(\"scrapePool\")\n\tstate := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"state\"))\n\tshowActive := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"active\"\n\tshowDropped := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"dropped\"\n\tres := &TargetDiscovery{}\n\tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n\n\tif showActive {\n\t\ttargetsActive := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive()\n\t\tactivePools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsActive)\n\t\tres.ActiveTargets = make([]*Target, 0, numTargets)\n\n\t\tfor _, pool := range activePools {\n\t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsActive[pool] {\n\t\t\t\tlastErrStr := \"\"\n\t\t\t\tlastErr := target.LastError()\n\t\t\t\tif lastErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlastErrStr = lastErr.Error()\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tglobalURL, err := getGlobalURL(target.URL(), api.globalURLOptions)\n\n\t\t\t\tres.ActiveTargets = append(res.ActiveTargets, &Target{\n\t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:           target.Labels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeURL:        target.URL().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tGlobalURL:        globalURL.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError: func() string {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase err == nil && lastErrStr == \"\":\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase err != nil:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s: %w\", lastErrStr, err).Error()\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn lastErrStr\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastScrape:         target.LastScrape(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastScrapeDuration: target.LastScrapeDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:             target.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeInterval:     target.GetValue(model.ScrapeIntervalLabel),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeTimeout:      target.GetValue(model.ScrapeTimeoutLabel),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tres.ActiveTargets = []*Target{}\n\t}\n\tif showDropped {\n\t\tres.DroppedTargetCounts = api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDroppedCounts()\n\n\t\ttargetsDropped := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDropped()\n\t\tdroppedPools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsDropped)\n\t\tres.DroppedTargets = make([]*DroppedTarget, 0, numTargets)\n\t\tfor _, pool := range droppedPools {\n\t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsDropped[pool] {\n\t\t\t\tres.DroppedTargets = append(res.DroppedTargets, &DroppedTarget{\n\t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tres.DroppedTargets = []*DroppedTarget{}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc matchLabels(lset labels.Labels, matchers []*labels.Matcher) bool {\n\tfor _, m := range matchers {\n\t\tif !m.Matches(lset.Get(m.Name)) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) targetMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmatchTarget := r.FormValue(\"match_target\")\n\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\tvar err error\n\tif matchTarget != \"\" {\n\t\tmatchers, err = parser.ParseMetricSelector(matchTarget)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match_target\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n\tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n\tres := []metricMetadata{}\n\tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n\t\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttargetLabels := t.Labels(builder)\n\t\t\t// Filter targets that don't satisfy the label matchers.\n\t\t\tif matchTarget != \"\" && !matchLabels(targetLabels, matchers) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If no metric is specified, get the full list for the target.\n\t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, md := range t.ListMetadata() {\n\t\t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tTarget:       targetLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMetricFamily: md.MetricFamily,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tType:         md.Type,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tHelp:         md.Help,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tUnit:         md.Unit,\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Get metadata for the specified metric.\n\t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n\t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n\t\t\t\t\tTarget: targetLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:   md.Type,\n\t\t\t\t\tHelp:   md.Help,\n\t\t\t\t\tUnit:   md.Unit,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\ntype metricMetadata struct {\n\tTarget       labels.Labels    `json:\"target\"`\n\tMetricFamily string           `json:\"metric,omitempty\"`\n\tType         model.MetricType `json:\"type\"`\n\tHelp         string           `json:\"help\"`\n\tUnit         string           `json:\"unit\"`\n}\n\n// AlertmanagerDiscovery has all the active Alertmanagers.\ntype AlertmanagerDiscovery struct {\n\tActiveAlertmanagers  []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"activeAlertmanagers\"`\n\tDroppedAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"droppedAlertmanagers\"`\n}\n\n// AlertmanagerTarget has info on one AM.\ntype AlertmanagerTarget struct {\n\tURL string `json:\"url\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) alertmanagers(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\turls := api.alertmanagerRetriever(r.Context()).Alertmanagers()\n\tdroppedURLS := api.alertmanagerRetriever(r.Context()).DroppedAlertmanagers()\n\tams := &AlertmanagerDiscovery{ActiveAlertmanagers: make([]*AlertmanagerTarget, len(urls)), DroppedAlertmanagers: make([]*AlertmanagerTarget, len(droppedURLS))}\n\tfor i, url := range urls {\n\t\tams.ActiveAlertmanagers[i] = &AlertmanagerTarget{URL: url.String()}\n\t}\n\tfor i, url := range droppedURLS {\n\t\tams.DroppedAlertmanagers[i] = &AlertmanagerTarget{URL: url.String()}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{ams, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// AlertDiscovery has info for all active alerts.\ntype AlertDiscovery struct {\n\tAlerts []*Alert `json:\"alerts\"`\n}\n\n// Alert has info for an alert.\ntype Alert struct {\n\tLabels          labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n\tAnnotations     labels.Labels `json:\"annotations\"`\n\tState           string        `json:\"state\"`\n\tActiveAt        *time.Time    `json:\"activeAt,omitempty\"`\n\tKeepFiringSince *time.Time    `json:\"keepFiringSince,omitempty\"`\n\tValue           string        `json:\"value\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) alerts(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\talertingRules := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).AlertingRules()\n\talerts := []*Alert{}\n\n\tfor _, alertingRule := range alertingRules {\n\t\talerts = append(\n\t\t\talerts,\n\t\t\trulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(alertingRule.ActiveAlerts())...,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\tres := &AlertDiscovery{Alerts: alerts}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rulesAlerts []*rules.Alert) []*Alert {\n\tapiAlerts := make([]*Alert, len(rulesAlerts))\n\tfor i, ruleAlert := range rulesAlerts {\n\t\tapiAlerts[i] = &Alert{\n\t\t\tLabels:      ruleAlert.Labels,\n\t\t\tAnnotations: ruleAlert.Annotations,\n\t\t\tState:       ruleAlert.State.String(),\n\t\t\tActiveAt:    &ruleAlert.ActiveAt,\n\t\t\tValue:       strconv.FormatFloat(ruleAlert.Value, 'e', -1, 64),\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tapiAlerts[i].KeepFiringSince = &ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiAlerts\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) metricMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tmetrics := map[string]map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tlimitPerMetric := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit_per_metric\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limitPerMetric, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit_per_metric must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n\tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n\t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, mm := range t.ListMetadata() {\n\t\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: mm.Type, Help: mm.Help, Unit: mm.Unit}\n\t\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[mm.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[mm.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: md.Type, Help: md.Help, Unit: md.Unit}\n\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[md.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[md.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n\tres := map[string][]metadata.Metadata{}\n\tfor name, set := range metrics {\n\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ts := []metadata.Metadata{}\n\t\tfor metadata := range set {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, metadata)\n\t\t}\n\t\tres[name] = s\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\ntype RuleDiscovery struct {\n\tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n\tGroupNextToken string       `json:\"groupNextToken,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// RuleGroup has info for rules which are part of a group.\ntype RuleGroup struct {\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\tFile string `json:\"file\"`\n\t// In order to preserve rule ordering, while exposing type (alerting or recording)\n\t// specific properties, both alerting and recording rules are exposed in the\n\t// same array.\n\tRules          []Rule    `json:\"rules\"`\n\tInterval       float64   `json:\"interval\"`\n\tLimit          int       `json:\"limit\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64   `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n}\n\ntype Rule any\n\ntype AlertingRule struct {\n\t// State can be \"pending\", \"firing\", \"inactive\".\n\tState          string           `json:\"state\"`\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tDuration       float64          `json:\"duration\"`\n\tKeepFiringFor  float64          `json:\"keepFiringFor\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels\"`\n\tAnnotations    labels.Labels    `json:\"annotations\"`\n\tAlerts         []*Alert         `json:\"alerts\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of an alertingRule is always \"alerting\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\ntype RecordingRule struct {\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of a recordingRule is always \"recording\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) rules(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tqueryFormToSet := func(values []string) map[string]struct{} {\n\t\tset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(values))\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tset[v] = struct{}{}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn set\n\t}\n\n\trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n\trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n\tfSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"file[]\"])\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\truleGroups := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).RuleGroups()\n\tres := &RuleDiscovery{RuleGroups: make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))}\n\ttyp := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\"))\n\n\tif typ != \"\" && typ != \"alert\" && typ != \"record\" {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"not supported value %q\", typ), \"type\")\n\t}\n\n\treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n\treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n\t}\n\n\tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r)\n\tif parseErr != nil {\n\t\treturn *parseErr\n\t}\n\n\trgs := make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))\n\n\tfoundToken := false\n\n\tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfoundToken = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(fSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := fSet[grp.File()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tapiRuleGroup := &RuleGroup{\n\t\t\tName:           grp.Name(),\n\t\t\tFile:           grp.File(),\n\t\t\tInterval:       grp.Interval().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLimit:          grp.Limit(),\n\t\t\tRules:          []Rule{},\n\t\t\tEvaluationTime: grp.GetEvaluationTime().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLastEvaluation: grp.GetLastEvaluation(),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n\t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n\n\t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n\t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts []*Alert\n\t\t\t\tif !excludeAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tactiveAlerts = rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rule.ActiveAlerts())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = AlertingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tState:          rule.State().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tDuration:       rule.HoldDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tKeepFiringFor:  rule.KeepFiringFor().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAnnotations:    rule.Annotations(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAlerts:         activeAlerts,\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"alerting\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcase *rules.RecordingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnRecording {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = RecordingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"recording\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"failed to assert type of rule '%v'\", rule.Name())\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n\t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n\t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n\t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. Therefore a next token is required.\n\t\t\t\tres.GroupNextToken = getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name())\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trgs = append(rgs, apiRuleGroup)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"invalid group_next_token '%v'. were rule groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n\t}\n\n\tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n\texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n\n\tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := strconv.ParseBool(excludeAlertsParam)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"error converting exclude_alerts: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn excludeAlerts, nil\n}\n\nfunc parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r *http.Request) (int64, string, *apiFuncResult) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups int64 = -1\n\t\terr             error\n\t)\n\tmaxGroups := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_limit\")\n\tnextToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_next_token\")\n\n\tif nextToken != \"\" && maxGroups == \"\" {\n\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be present in order to paginate over the groups\"), \"group_next_token\")\n\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups, err = strconv.ParseInt(maxGroups, 10, 32)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"group_limit needs to be a valid number: %w\", err), \"group_limit\")\n\t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parsedMaxGroups <= 0 {\n\t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be greater than 0\"), \"group_limit\")\n\t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif parsedMaxGroups > 0 {\n\t\treturn parsedMaxGroups, nextToken, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn -1, \"\", nil\n}\n\nfunc getRuleGroupNextToken(file, group string) string {\n\th := sha1.New()\n\th.Write([]byte(file + \";\" + group))\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n}\n\ntype prometheusConfig struct {\n\tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstatus, err := api.runtimeInfo()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{status, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{status, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveBuildInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{api.buildInfo, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveConfig(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tcfg := &prometheusConfig{\n\t\tYAML: api.config().String(),\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{cfg, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveFlags(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{api.flagsMap, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// TSDBStat holds the information about individual cardinality.\ntype TSDBStat struct {\n\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n\tValue uint64 QueryFunc processes PromQL queries.\ntype QueryFunc func(ctx context.Context, q string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error)\n\n// EngineQueryFunc returns a new query function that executes instant queries against\n// the given engine.\n// It converts scalar into vector results.\nfunc EngineQueryFunc(engine promql.QueryEngine, q storage.Queryable) QueryFunc {\n\treturn func(ctx context.Context, qs string, t time.Time) (promql.Vector, error) {\n\t\tq, err := engine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, q, nil, qs, t)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tres := q.Exec(ctx)\n\t\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, res.Err\n\t\t}\n\t\tswitch v := res.Value.(type) {\n\t\tcase promql.Vector:\n\t\t\treturn v, nil\n\t\tcase promql.Scalar:\n\t\t\treturn promql.Vector{promql.Sample{\n\t\t\t\tT:      v.T,\n\t\t\t\tF:      v.V,\n\t\t\t\tMetric: labels.Labels{},\n\t\t\t}}, nil\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"rule result is not a vector or scalar\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// DefaultEvalIterationFunc is the default implementation of\n// GroupEvalIterationFunc that is periodically invoked to evaluate the rules\n// in a group at a given point in time and updates Group state and metrics\n// accordingly. Custom GroupEvalIterationFunc implementations are recommended\n// to invoke this function as well, to ensure correct Group state and metrics\n// are maintained.\nfunc DefaultEvalIterationFunc(ctx context.Context, g *Group, evalTimestamp time.Time) {\n\tg.metrics.IterationsScheduled.WithLabelValues(GroupKey(g.file, g.name)).Inc()\n\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tg.Eval(ctx, evalTimestamp)\n\ttimeSinceStart := time.Since(start)\n\n\tg.metrics.IterationDuration.Observe(timeSinceStart.Seconds())\n\tg.updateRuleEvaluationTimeSum()\n\tg.setEvaluationTime(timeSinceStart)\n\tg.setLastEvaluation(start)\n\tg.setLastEvalTimestamp(evalTimestamp)\n}\n\n// The Manager manages recording and alerting rules.\ntype Manager struct {\n\topts                 *ManagerOptions\n\tgroups               map[string]*Group\n\tmtx                  sync.RWMutex\n\tblock                chan struct{}\n\tdone                 chan struct{}\n\trestored             bool\n\trestoreNewRuleGroups bool\n\n\tlogger *slog.Logger\n}\n\n// NotifyFunc sends notifications about a set of alerts generated by the given expression.\ntype NotifyFunc func(ctx context.Context, expr string, alerts ...*Alert)\n\n// ManagerOptions bundles options for the Manager.\ntype ManagerOptions struct {\n\tNameValidationScheme      model.ValidationScheme\n\tExternalURL               *url.URL\n\tQueryFunc                 QueryFunc\n\tNotifyFunc                NotifyFunc\n\tContext                   context.Context\n\tAppendable                storage.Appendable\n\tQueryable                 storage.Queryable\n\tLogger                    *slog.Logger\n\tRegisterer                prometheus.Registerer\n\tOutageTolerance           time.Duration\n\tForGracePeriod            time.Duration\n\tResendDelay               time.Duration\n\tGroupLoader               GroupLoader\n\tDefaultRuleQueryOffset    func() time.Duration\n\tMaxConcurrentEvals        int64\n\tConcurrentEvalsEnabled    bool\n\tRuleConcurrencyController RuleConcurrencyController\n\tRuleDependencyController  RuleDependencyController\n\t// At present, manager only restores `for` state when manager is newly created which happens\n\t// during restarts. This flag provides an option to restore the `for` state when new rule groups are\n\t// added to an existing manager\n\tRestoreNewRuleGroups bool\n\n\tMetrics *Metrics\n}\n\n// NewManager returns an implementation of Manager, ready to be started\n// by calling the Run method.\nfunc NewManager(o *ManagerOptions) *Manager {\n\tswitch o.NameValidationScheme {\n\tcase model.UTF8Validation, model.LegacyValidation:\n\tcase model.UnsetValidation:\n\t\to.NameValidationScheme = model.UTF8Validation\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unrecognized name validation scheme: %s\", o.NameValidationScheme))\n\t}\n\tif o.Context == nil {\n\t\to.Context = context.Background()\n\t}\n\n\tif o.Metrics == nil {\n\t\to.Metrics = NewGroupMetrics(o.Registerer)\n\t}\n\n\tif o.GroupLoader == nil {\n\t\to.GroupLoader = FileLoader{}\n\t}\n\n\tif o.RuleConcurrencyController == nil {\n\t\tif o.ConcurrentEvalsEnabled {\n\t\t\to.RuleConcurrencyController = newRuleConcurrencyController(o.MaxConcurrentEvals)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\to.RuleConcurrencyController = sequentialRuleEvalController{}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif o.RuleDependencyController == nil {\n\t\to.RuleDependencyController = ruleDependencyController{}\n\t}\n\n\tif o.Logger == nil {\n\t\to.Logger = promslog.NewNopLogger()\n\t}\n\n\tm := &Manager{\n\t\tgroups:               map[string]*Group{},\n\t\topts:                 o,\n\t\tblock:                make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tdone:                 make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tlogger:               o.Logger,\n\t\trestoreNewRuleGroups: o.RestoreNewRuleGroups,\n\t}\n\n\treturn m\n}\n\n// Run starts processing of the rule manager. It is blocking.\nfunc (m *Manager) Run() {\n\tm.logger.Info(\"Starting rule manager...\")\n\tm.start()\n\t<-m.done\n}\n\nfunc (m *Manager) start() {\n\tclose(m.block)\n}\n\n// Stop the rule manager's rule evaluation cycles.\nfunc (m *Manager) Stop() {\n\tm.mtx.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mtx.Unlock()\n\n\tm.logger.Info(\"Stopping rule manager...\")\n\n\t// Stop all groups asynchronously, then wait for them to finish.\n\t// This is faster than stopping and waiting for each group in sequence.\n\tfor _, eg := range m.groups {\n\t\teg.stopAsync()\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, eg := range m.groups {\n\t\teg.waitStopped()\n\t}\n\n\t// Shut down the groups waiting multiple evaluation intervals to write\n\t// staleness markers.\n\tclose(m.done)\n\n\tm.logger.Info(\"Rule manager stopped\")\n}\n\n// Update the rule manager's state as the config requires. If\n// loading the new rules failed the old rule set is restored.\n// This method will no-op in case the manager is already stopped.\nfunc (m *Manager) Update(interval time.Duration, files []string, externalLabels labels.Labels, externalURL string, groupEvalIterationFunc GroupEvalIterationFunc) error {\n\tm.mtx.Lock()\n\tdefer m.mtx.Unlock()\n\n\t// We cannot update a stopped manager\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-m.done:\n\t\treturn nil\n\tdefault:\n\t}\n\n\tgroups, errs := m.LoadGroups(interval, externalLabels, externalURL, groupEvalIterationFunc, false, files...)\n\n\tif errs != nil {\n\t\tfor _, e := range errs {\n\t\t\tm.logger.Error(\"loading groups failed\", \"err\", e)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"error loading rules, previous rule set restored\")\n\t}\n\tm.restored = true\n\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tfor _, newg := range groups {\n\t\t// If there is an old group with the same identifier,\n\t\t// check if new group equals with the old group, if yes then skip it.\n\t\t// If not equals, stop it and wait for it to finish the current iteration.\n\t\t// Then copy it into the new group.\n\t\tgn := GroupKey(newg.file, newg.name)\n\t\toldg, ok := m.groups[gn]\n\t\tdelete(m.groups, gn)\n\n\t\tif ok && oldg.Equals(newg) {\n\t\t\tgroups[gn] = oldg\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(newg *Group) {\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\toldg.stop()\n\t\t\t\tnewg.CopyState(oldg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t\t// Wait with starting evaluation until the rule manager\n\t\t\t// is told to run. This is necessary to avoid running\n\t\t\t// queries against a bootstrapping storage.\n\t\t\t<-m.block\n\t\t\tnewg.run(m.opts.Context)\n\t\t}(newg)\n\t}\n\n\t// Stop remaining old groups.\n\twg.Add(len(m.groups))\n\tfor n, oldg := range m.groups {\n\t\tgo func(n string, g *Group) {\n\t\t\tg.markStale = true\n\t\t\tg.stop()\n\t\t\tif m := g.metrics; m != nil {\n\t\t\t\tm.IterationsMissed.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.IterationsScheduled.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.EvalTotal.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.EvalFailures.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.GroupInterval.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.GroupLastEvalTime.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.GroupLastDuration.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.GroupRules.DeleteLabelValues(n)\n\t\t\t\tm.GroupSamples.DeleteLabelValues((n))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t}(n, oldg)\n\t}\n\n\twg.Wait()\n\tm.groups = groups\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// GroupLoader is responsible for loading rule groups from arbitrary sources and parsing them.\ntype GroupLoader interface {\n\tLoad(identifier string, ignoreUnknownFields bool, nameValidationScheme model.ValidationScheme) (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error)\n\tParse(query string) (parser.Expr, error)\n}\n\n// FileLoader is the default GroupLoader implementation. It defers to rulefmt.ParseFile\n// and parser.ParseExpr.\ntype FileLoader struct{}\n\nfunc (FileLoader) Load(identifier string, ignoreUnknownFields bool, nameValidationScheme model.ValidationScheme) (*rulefmt.RuleGroups, []error) {\n\treturn rulefmt.ParseFile(identifier, ignoreUnknownFields, nameValidationScheme)\n}\n\nfunc (FileLoader) Parse(query string) (parser.Expr, error) { return parser.ParseExpr(query) }\n\n// LoadGroups reads groups from a list of files.\nfunc (m *Manager) LoadGroups(\n\tinterval time.Duration, externalLabels labels.Labels, externalURL string, groupEvalIterationFunc GroupEvalIterationFunc, ignoreUnknownFields bool, filenames ...string,\n) (map[string]*Group, []error) {\n\tgroups := make(map[string]*Group)\n\n\tshouldRestore := !m.restored || m.restoreNewRuleGroups\n\n\tfor _, fn := range filenames {\n\t\trgs, errs := m.opts.GroupLoader.Load(fn, ignoreUnknownFields, m.opts.NameValidationScheme)\n\t\tif errs != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errs\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, rg := range rgs.Groups {\n\t\t\titv := interval\n\t\t\tif rg.Interval != 0 {\n\t\t\t\titv = time.Duration(rg.Interval)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\trules := make([]Rule, 0, len(rg.Rules))\n\t\t\tfor _, r := range rg.Rules {\n\t\t\t\texpr, err := m.opts.GroupLoader.Parse(r.Expr)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, []error{fmt.Errorf(\"%s: %w\", fn, err)}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tmLabels := FromMaps(rg.Labels, r.Labels)\n\n\t\t\t\tif r.Alert != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\trules = append(rules, NewAlertingRule(\n\t\t\t\t\t\tr.Alert,\n\t\t\t\t\t\texpr,\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttime.Duration(r.For),\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttime.Duration(r.KeepFiringFor),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tlabels.FromMap(r.Annotations),\n\t\t\t\t\t\texternalLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\t\texternalURL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t!shouldRestore,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tm.logger.With(\"alert\", r.Alert),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\trules = append(rules, NewRecordingRule(\n\t\t\t\t\tr.Record,\n\t\t\t\t\texpr,\n\t\t\t\t\tmLabels,\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check dependencies between rules and store it on the Rule itself.\n\t\t\tm.opts.RuleDependencyController.AnalyseRules(rules)\n\n\t\t\tgroups[GroupKey(fn, rg.Name)] = NewGroup(GroupOptions{\n\t\t\t\tName:              rg.Name,\n\t\t\t\tFile:              fn,\n\t\t\t\tInterval:          itv,\n\t\t\t\tLimit:             rg.Limit,\n\t\t\t\tRules:             rules,\n\t\t\t\tShouldRestore:     shouldRestore,\n\t\t\t\tOpts:              m.opts,\n\t\t\t\tQueryOffset:       (*time.Duration)(rg.QueryOffset),\n\t\t\t\tdone:              m.done,\n\t\t\t\tEvalIterationFunc: groupEvalIterationFunc,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn groups, nil\n}\n\n// RuleGroups returns the list of manager's rule groups.\nfunc (m *Manager) RuleGroups() []*Group {\n\tm.mtx.RLock()\n\tdefer m.mtx.RUnlock()\n\n\trgs := make([]*Group, 0, len(m.groups))\n\tfor _, g := range m.groups {\n\t\trgs = append(rgs, g)\n\t}\n\n\tslices.SortFunc(rgs, func(a, b *Group) int {\n\t\tfileCompare := strings.Compare(a.file, b.file)\n\n\t\t// If its 0, then the file names are the same.\n\t\t// Lets look at the group names in that case.\n\t\tif fileCompare != 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fileCompare\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn strings.Compare(a.name, b.name)\n\t})\n\n\treturn rgs\n}\n\n// Rules returns the list of the manager's rules.\nfunc (m *Manager) Rules(matcherSets ...[]*labels.Matcher) []Rule {\n\tm.mtx.RLock()\n\tdefer m.mtx.RUnlock()\n\n\tvar rules []Rule\n\tfor _, g := range m.groups {\n\t\trules = append(rules, g.Rules(matcherSets...)...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn rules\n}\n\n// AlertingRules returns the list of the manager's alerting rules.\nfunc (m *Manager) AlertingRules() []*AlertingRule {\n\talerts := []*AlertingRule{}\n\tfor _, rule := range m.Rules() {\n\t\tif alertingRule, ok := rule.(*AlertingRule); ok {\n\t\t\talerts = append(alerts, alertingRule)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn alerts\n}\n\ntype Sender interface {\n\tSend(alerts ...*notifier.Alert)\n}\n\n// SendAlerts implements the rules.NotifyFunc for a Notifier.\nfunc SendAlerts(s Sender, externalURL string) NotifyFunc {\n\treturn func(_ context.Context, expr string, alerts ...*Alert) {\n\t\tvar res []*notifier.Alert\n\n\t\tfor _, alert := range alerts {\n\t\t\ta := &notifier.Alert{\n\t\t\t\tStartsAt:     alert.FiredAt,\n\t\t\t\tLabels:       alert.Labels,\n\t\t\t\tAnnotations:  alert.Annotations,\n\t\t\t\tGeneratorURL: externalURL + strutil.TableLinkForExpression(expr),\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !alert.ResolvedAt.IsZero() {\n\t\t\t\ta.EndsAt = alert.ResolvedAt\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\ta.EndsAt = alert.ValidUntil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tres = append(res, a)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(alerts) > 0 {\n\t\t\ts.Send(res...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// RuleDependencyController controls whether a set of rules have dependencies between each other.\ntype RuleDependencyController interface {\n\t// AnalyseRules analyses dependencies between the input rules. For each rule that it's guaranteed\n\t// not having any dependants and/or dependency, this function should call Rule.SetDependentRules(...)\n\t// and/or Rule.SetDependencyRules(...).\n\tAnalyseRules(rules []Rule)\n}\n\ntype ruleDependencyController struct{}\n\n// AnalyseRules implements RuleDependencyController.\nfunc (ruleDependencyController) AnalyseRules(rules []Rule) {\n\tdepMap := buildDependencyMap(rules)\n\n\tif depMap == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, r := range rules {\n\t\tr.SetDependentRules(depMap.dependents(r))\n\t\tr.SetDependencyRules(depMap.dependencies(r))\n\t}\n}\n\n// ConcurrentRules represents a slice of indexes of rules that can be evaluated concurrently.\ntype ConcurrentRules []int\n\n// RuleConcurrencyController controls concurrency for rules that are safe to be evaluated concurrently.\n// Its purpose is to bound the amount of concurrency in rule evaluations to avoid overwhelming the Prometheus\n// server with additional query load. Concurrency is controlled globally, not on a per-group basis.\ntype RuleConcurrencyController interface {\n\t// SplitGroupIntoBatches returns an ordered slice of ConcurrentRules, which are batches of rules that can be evaluated concurrently.\n\t// The rules are represented by their index from the input rule group.\n\tSplitGroupIntoBatches(ctx context.Context, group *Group) []ConcurrentRules\n\n\t// Allow determines if the given rule is allowed to be evaluated concurrently.\n\t// If Allow() returns true, then Done() must be called to release the acquired slot and corresponding cleanup is done.\n\t// It is important that both *Group and Rule are not retained and only be used for the duration of the call.\n\tAllow(ctx context.Context, group *Group, rule Rule) bool\n\n\t// Done releases a concurrent evaluation slot.\n\tDone(ctx context.Context)\n}\n\n// concurrentRuleEvalController holds a weighted semaphore which controls the concurrent evaluation of rules.\ntype concurrentRuleEvalController struct {\n\tsema *semaphore.Weighted\n}\n\nfunc newRuleConcurrencyController(maxConcurrency int64) RuleConcurrencyController {\n\treturn &concurrentRuleEvalController{\n\t\tsema: semaphore.NewWeighted(maxConcurrency),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (c *concurrentRuleEvalController) Allow(context.Context, *Group, Rule) bool {\n\treturn c.sema.TryAcquire(1)\n}\n\nfunc (*concurrentRuleEvalController) SplitGroupIntoBatches(_ context.Context, g *Group) []ConcurrentRules {\n\t// Using the rule dependency controller information (rules being identified as having no dependencies or no dependants),\n\t// we can safely run the following concurrent groups:\n\t// 1. Concurrently, all rules that have no dependencies\n\t// 2. Sequentially, all rules that have both dependencies and dependants\n\t// 3. Concurrently, all rules that have no dependants\n\n\tvar noDependencies []int\n\tvar dependenciesAndDependants []int\n\tvar noDependants []int\n\n\tfor i, r := range g.rules {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase r.NoDependencyRules():\n\t\t\tnoDependencies = append(noDependencies, i)\n\t\tcase !r.NoDependentRules() && !r.NoDependencyRules():\n\t\t\tdependenciesAndDependants = append(dependenciesAndDependants, i)\n\t\tcase r.NoDependentRules():\n\t\t\tnoDependants = append(noDependants, i)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar order []ConcurrentRules\n\tif len(noDependencies) > 0 {\n\t\torder = append(order, noDependencies)\n\t}\n\tfor _, r := range dependenciesAndDependants {\n\t\torder = append(order, []int{r})\n\t}\n\tif len(noDependants) > 0 {\n\t\torder = append(order, noDependants)\n\t}\n\n\treturn order\n}\n\nfunc (c *concurrentRuleEvalController) Done(context.Context) {\n\tc.sema.Release(1)\n}\n\nvar _ RuleConcurrencyController = &sequentialRuleEvalController{}\n\n// sequentialRuleEvalController is a RuleConcurrencyController that runs every rule sequentially.\ntype sequentialRuleEvalController struct{}\n\nfunc (sequentialRuleEvalController) Allow(context.Context,
